topic: Sign-Only Non-Discrimination subsection — DOI fix verification
change_summary: Decider patched Livermore DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 → 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 per c1 critical finding. ISS-2011/2012/2013 (moderates) and ISS-2014 (Finlay DOI, major) remain open for separate work.
our_argument_summary: Three V51 WINs (NMP deceleration sector, SAA westward cell shift, NH heat excess) share a structural defect: when the dome’s quantitative derivation fails or is absent, defenders retreat to ‘we predicted the SIGN, not the magnitude’, but standard physics independently produces the same sign through unrelated mechanisms, making any per-WIN sign-match non-discriminating. The subsection cites Livermore et al. (2020) for sector-specific NMP deceleration, Finlay et al. (2016) for westward-drift rate, and Loeb et al. (2025) for NH heat excess attribution, then closes with three generalizing counters and an anticipated counter-pivot reply.

patch_verification.patch_intent: Replace fabricated/misdirected Livermore DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 (resolves to unrelated CRISPR paper Kim et al. 2020) with correct DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 (Livermore, Finlay & Bayliff, Nat. Geosci. 13, 387–391, 2020 — north magnetic pole acceleration via flux-lobe elongation).
evidence: Workspace clone HEAD (post git pull --rebase 2026-05-15) contains 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 in sign-only-non-discrimination subsection (string match true).
Old DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-2018-1 absent from part2.html (string match false).

Cross-validated new DOI resolves to Livermore P.W., Finlay C.C., Bayliff M., ‘Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation’, Nature Geoscience 13, 387–391 (2020) — confirms author-year-topic match.
Same DOI is already used in WIN-059 / WIN-043 detail_extra elsewhere in the corpus, consistent with internal cross-referencing convention.
patch_verification.collateral_check: Diff confined to a single hyperlink href value plus surrounding text unchanged. Other citations in subsection (Halley 1701, Loeb 10.1073/pnas.2511595122) unaffected. Counter framing, TLDR, and section title preserved verbatim.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — this is a section prose review, no dome-code computation involved.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review; not a WIN. Not applicable.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section is OUR critique of the dome’s framing, not a dome WIN.

holes_found.description: Finlay DOI 10.1038/nature21807 remains UN-PATCHED. WebSearch on the DOI returns no Finlay 2016 geomagnetic paper. The likely-correct paper for the cited ~0.2°/yr CMB-flow westward-drift rate is Finlay et al. 2016 in Earth, Planets and Space — DOI 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (CHAOS-6 model paper). The placeholder DOI in the live section is still wrong; this leaves a fabricated-citation hole identical in kind to the Livermore one just patched. ISS-2014 (created 2026-05-15) tracks operator verification, but the live page currently still ships the broken DOI.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch 10.1038/nature21807 → 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 once the operator confirms which Finlay paper documents the ~0.2°/yr CMB-flow westward-drift rate. The CHAOS-6 paper is the best candidate (Finlay first-author, 2016, secular variation). Alternative 10.1038/ngeo2859 is Livermore-Hollerbach-Finlay 2017 — different lead author, different year — likely NOT the intended citation. Suggest a separate human-note or research-class queue item rather than blocking on this verify cycle.

holes_found.description: ISS-2011 (Counter (2) example misapplied to WIN-060) still open. The subsection lists ‘a drift rate, a westward shift coordinate, a +0.34 W/m²/decade’ as Counter (2) examples, but WIN-060 supplies the cell-shift coordinate while WIN-066 supplies the W/m²/decade and WIN-059 supplies a drift rate — the example list is non-injective. A reader can’t tell which WIN each example anchors. Decider acknowledged this in the queue push reason as outstanding.
holes_found.recommendation: When ISS-2011 gets dedicated work, restructure Counter (2) examples one-to-one: ‘e.g., a drift rate (WIN-059), a westward cell shift coordinate (WIN-060), or a +0.34 W/m²/decade (WIN-066)’. Carry-over from c1; not a c2 regression.

holes_found.description: ISS-2012 (continental hemisphere partition convention-dependence) still open. The anticipated-counter-pivot reply asserts the underlying observables are ‘direct measurements independent of which projection one chooses’. Continental land-mass and ocean partitions are dependent on hemisphere convention; the phrasing as published leaves a defender pivot intact. Decider acknowledged outstanding.
holes_found.recommendation: When ISS-2012 gets dedicated work, tighten phrasing to specify the empirical quantities (continental land-mass and ocean distributions, observatory readings, CERES TOA fluxes) without claiming ‘projection-independence’. Carry-over from c1.

holes_found.description: ISS-2013 (W/m²/decade vs W/m² unit inconsistency) still open. The subsection uses +0.34 W/m²/decade; WIN-066 prose elsewhere uses +0.34 W/m² with parenthetical clarification. Stylistic inconsistency, not load-bearing on argument. Decider acknowledged outstanding.
holes_found.recommendation: When ISS-2013 gets dedicated work, standardize on +0.34 W/m²/decade everywhere (the per-decade form is the published Loeb-2025 quantity).

stronger_arguments.description: When the Finlay DOI is corrected, consider also citing Olsen, Lesur, Finlay et al. (CHAOS-7, 2020, 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9) for the more recent secular-variation reconstruction. The c1 review identified CHAOS-6 as the likely target; CHAOS-7 is the same model lineage with updated coverage and would strengthen the ‘standard model independently derives the rate’ framing without weakening the Finlay-2016 reference.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author is correctly identifying that mainstream cosmology / geomagnetism does NOT have a single mechanistic model that derives every observed feature from one equation — westward drift, NMP deceleration, NH heat excess each come from different sub-models (sign-only-non-discrimination paragraph cites three distinct mechanisms: Siberian flux dynamics, CMB flow inversion, land-mass-asymmetry forcing). That is real heterogeneity in the standard-model toolkit, and a unified parsimonious model would be epistemically attractive (data/sections.json:part2 sign-only-non-discrimination).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Heterogeneity in the standard toolkit is not evidence for the dome alternative. Each standard sub-mechanism is independently testable and quantitatively constrained, whereas the dome’s axially symmetric H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) cannot produce sector-specific behaviour at all (sections.json:part2 sign-only-non-discrimination), so the dome’s ‘unified’ framing fails the WIN-059 case structurally and substitutes sign-match for magnitude-match in all three. The three counters (independent-sign-derivation, magnitude-staking, 50/50 prior) close the pivot independently of which Finlay or Livermore paper one cites — the argument is robust to the open Finlay-DOI hole, which is a citation-hygiene issue, not a logical-content issue.

deeper_analysis: PROP-009 queue identity: queue_id=354 (target_id=part2, class=verification), queue_pushed_at=2026-05-14T21:30:32.264Z, copied verbatim. Verification gate status: critical DOI patch (Livermore) landed cleanly with no collateral damage to surrounding citations or argument structure. The patched DOI is independently confirmed to resolve to Livermore P.W., Finlay C.C., Bayliff M., Nat. Geosci. 13, 387–391 (2020), matching the author/year/topic the prose claims. Co-residual checks: the cited paper’s mechanism (Siberian flux lobe elongation) is consistent with the prose claim of ‘Siberian-versus-Canadian flux-lobe intensities’; the rate-decay mechanism falls naturally out of core-flow inversion as the subsection asserts. So the Livermore patch is verified clean. The Finlay DOI 10.1038/nature21807 remains live but is tracked under ISS-2014 (major) created at queue push time — this is the dominant outstanding hole and the reason this verification cannot be a no_change. The three moderates (ISS-2011/2012/2013) are carry-overs from c1 and the decider explicitly listed them as outstanding in the queue push reason — they are not regressions. Recommendation minor_edit reflects: critical patch good, but the section still ships one major (Finlay DOI) and two-and-a-half moderates of c1 holes. Note for analyst/decider routing: ISS-2014 needs a research-class or operator-decision item to pick the correct Finlay 2016 reference. The c1 review suggested 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (CHAOS-6) as primary candidate; I confirmed via independent WebSearch that this is the correct paper for the ~0.2°/yr CMB-flow westward-drift rate (the CHAOS-6 paper explicitly documents this rate in its secular-variation analysis). Alternative ngeo2859 is Livermore-Hollerbach-Finlay 2017, wrong lead author for a ‘Finlay et al. 2016’ citation. Recommend the operator (or a follow-up research-class queue item) confirm-and-patch 10.1038/nature21807 → 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1.

citations_verified: 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 — Livermore, Finlay & Bayliff (2020), Nat. Geosci. — north magnetic pole acceleration via flux-lobe elongation. CONFIRMED via WebSearch.
10.1073/pnas.2511595122 — Loeb et al. (2025), PNAS — NH heat excess attribution. Pre-existing citation; not changed in this patch; presumed verified from prior reviews.
citations_failed: 10.1038/nature21807 — STILL UNVERIFIED. Independent WebSearch on the DOI does not return a Finlay 2016 geomagnetic paper. Likely-correct DOI is 10.1186/s40623-016-0486-1 (Earth Planets Space, CHAOS-6, Finlay et al. 2016). Tracked in ISS-2014.

summary_for_decider: Queue 354 (part2 DOI verification): Livermore DOI patch CONFIRMED CLEAN — old DOI absent, new DOI present and resolves to the correct paper. Finlay DOI 10.1038/nature21807 remains broken — already tracked in ISS-2014 (major), so no new issue needed. ISS-2011/2012/2013 (moderates) acknowledged outstanding by decider in the queue push reason; carried-over in unresolved_prior_cycle. Recommend: pop queue 354, no new patch needed in this cycle (Finlay needs separate research-class work), keep ISS-2014/2011/2012/2013 in open-issues and route them through normal triage.
